mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easing discount-rate pressure and supports equity multiples, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate is falling from recent highs.
failure_condition: The signal is rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, tighter financing conditions compress valuation multiples and raise recession odds, so the next 20 trading days of S&P 500 returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS indicates weakening household income and demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not outperform either UNRATE or PAYEMS alone, or if positive 20-day SPX returns still occur in more than 60% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in RSAFS reflects weakening real activity and retail demand, which reduces earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales contraction episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the signal has no incremental predictive value versus a null benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market reads the economy as facing stagflationary pressure, so equity returns over the next 60 trading days are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term does not dominate the single-series effects, or if low-INDPRO/high-CPI regimes are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising HOUST series marks housing demand stabilization and future construction activity, supporting cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if housing upturn episodes do not deliver positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, simultaneous acceleration of CPIAUCSL and a higher FEDFUNDS rate increases real-rate and policy-shock risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined CPI and FEDFUNDS interaction does not beat each input alone, or if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after inflation-and-rate accelerations.

mechanism_narrative: A downward revision path in GDPC1 indicates that reported output is weaker than initially believed, and in a post-revision recession scare this should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP weakness does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns in revision-sensitive periods, or if the signal remains positive across rolling out-of-sample windows.
